title: The Fisherman and the Magic Fish
content:
Once upon a time, a poor fisherman lived with his wife in a tiny cramped hut by the sea. Every day he went out in his small boat to fish, and every evening he returned with barely enough to eat. They had very little, but they made do, and for a long time they were content.

One morning the fisherman pulled up something extraordinary in his net: a large golden fish with dark, intelligent eyes. "Please," said the fish in a clear voice, "throw me back. I am an enchanted fish. Spare my life and I will grant you any wish you desire."

The fisherman was a gentle man. He tipped the fish back into the sea. "I want nothing," he said. "Go free." He went home and told his wife. She stared at him. "You wished for NOTHING? We live in a hut! Go back and ask for a proper cottage right now!"

Feeling ashamed, the fisherman went back and called for the fish. It appeared from the depths. He made his wife's wish. When he came home, there stood a neat cottage with a little garden. His wife was pleased, for a few days. Then she decided a cottage was not grand enough.

"Go back. Ask for a mansion." He went, and the fish granted it. A week later, she wanted a castle. Then she wanted to be a queen. Each time the fisherman trudged to the shore with a heavier heart. Each time, the sea grew a little darker and the waves a little rougher.

The fish granted every wish. The wife became a queen on a jeweled throne with a hundred servants. Still it was never enough. Her eyes roamed restlessly over everything she owned, always wanting more.

One morning before dawn she shook her husband awake. "I want to command the sun when to rise and the moon when to set! I want to be ruler of the entire universe!" The fisherman turned pale. "Please," he begged quietly. "Be thankful for what we have." But she would not listen.

The fisherman walked to the shore. The sea was black and churning violently. The sky was dark with storm clouds. He called for the fish with a trembling voice. The fish rose slowly from the dark, foaming waves.

"Go home," said the fish quietly. Then it slipped back beneath the surface and was gone. The fisherman turned and walked home. He opened the door and found the tiny cramped hut, exactly as it had always been. His wife sat in the corner in silence. Everything was gone. They had nothing again. And the magic fish was never seen again.

author: Brothers Grimm
